instructions:
Alabama requires fingerprinting for all resident producer applicants. Fingerprints must be electronically processed through Fieldprint.

Schedule your appointment at: https://fieldprintalabama.com/individuals

Important: Get fingerprinted AFTER passing the state exam and one day BEFORE applying for your license. Fingerprint results only remain in the state system for 30 days.

Fee: $48.25
Vendor: Fieldprint

Alabama requires 20 hours of pre-licensing education for Life insurance.

You must complete a state-approved pre-licensing course and pass the course final exam with 70% or higher to receive a certificate of completion before scheduling the state licensing exam.

Popular providers include ExamFX, Kaplan, and AD Banker.

Alabama insurance exams are administered by the University of Alabama in partnership with the Alabama Department of Insurance (ALDOI).

Schedule your exam at: https://training.ua.edu/insurance-testing/

Visit the ALDOI exam sites page and click on your preferred city to be directed to the University of Alabama registration page.

Exam info: https://aldoi.gov/licensing/examsites.aspx
override_description: Schedule exam via University of Alabama testing

instructions:
Alaska requires fingerprinting for all first-time resident insurance exam candidates.

Schedule fingerprinting through IdentoGO after scheduling your exam appointment with Pearson VUE.

Vendor: IdentoGO
Phone: (866) 761-8069
URL: https://www.identogo.com

Fee: ~$63.25 total ($15 IdentoGO + $48.25 Alaska Division of Insurance/FBI processing)

You may schedule fingerprinting at the same Pearson VUE exam center on the day of your exam.

Alaska requires 20 hours of pre-licensing education for Life insurance.

Complete a state-approved course and pass the course final exam before scheduling the state licensing exam.

Popular providers include ExamFX, Kaplan, and AD Banker.

Alaska insurance exams are administered by Pearson VUE.

Schedule online: https://www.pearsonvue.com
Phone: (800) 274-5993

Register at least 24 hours in advance of your desired exam date. Exams are available at Pearson VUE test centers.

Arizona requires fingerprinting for all first-time resident insurance exam candidates.

Do NOT schedule fingerprints until AFTER passing your licensing exam. All fees are non-refundable.

Step 1: Create an account with AZ Dept of Public Safety (AZDPS) at https://www.azdps.gov
- Select 'Background Check for Employment/Licensure'
- Use Fingerprint Code: INS-055.A
- Fee: $23 application fee

Step 2: Schedule fingerprinting with Fieldprint
- URL: https://www.fieldprintarizona.com
- Fee: $8.25

Total cost: ~$31.25

Arizona does NOT require pre-licensing education for insurance producers.

Pre-licensing courses are recommended but optional. Study materials and exam prep courses are available from providers like ExamFX, Kaplan, and AD Banker.

Arizona insurance exams are administered by PSI (effective September 2025).

Schedule online: https://test-takers.psiexams.com/anzins

Exams can be taken at a physical PSI testing center or remotely via PSI Bridge.

Arrive at least 30 minutes before your scheduled appointment. Bring valid government-issued photo ID.

Arkansas requires a criminal background check for all resident insurance license applicants.

Submit your background check and fees directly to the Arkansas State Police through the Arkansas Insurance Department's Criminal Background Check online portal.

Fee: $25

Keep the original receipt for your records and attach a copy to your license application.
override_description: Background check required via AR State Police ($25)

instructions:
Arkansas does NOT require pre-licensing education for Life insurance producers.

Pre-licensing courses are recommended but optional to help prepare for the state exam.

Arkansas insurance exams are administered by PSI.

Schedule online: https://www.psiexams.com
Phone: (855) 518-7458

Exams can be taken at a physical PSI testing center or remotely via online proctoring.

Life Insurance exam: 110 questions (100 scored, 10 pretest), 2.5 hours, passing score 70%.

California requires fingerprinting for all resident insurance license applicants.

Submit fingerprints AFTER filing your license application with the California Department of Insurance (CDI).

Option 1 - Capital Live Scan (CLS):
- Complete Form BCIA 8016 (Request for Live Scan Service)
- Fee: ~$59 ($17 FBI + $32 DOJ + $10 CDI contractor fee)

Option 2 - At PSI Test Centers:
- Available on exam day
- Fee: $68.95 ($17 FBI + $32 DOJ + $19.95 contractor fee)

URL: https://www.insurance.ca.gov/0200-industry/0200-prod-licensing/0100-applicant-info/0300-ca-fingerprint/
override_description: Fingerprinting required via Live Scan or PSI (~$59-$69)

instructions:
Effective January 1, 2026, California has ELIMINATED the pre-licensing education requirement for insurance producers.

Only 12 hours of Ethics and California Insurance Code instruction is required, including 1 hour of insurance fraud education.

URL: https://www.insurance.ca.gov/0200-industry/0030-seek-pre-lic/

California insurance exams are administered by PSI Services LLC.

Schedule online: https://www.psiexams.com
Phone: (833) 518-7456

PSI offers 20+ testing sites statewide. Fingerprinting can be obtained at PSI locations on exam day.

Exam convenience fee: $43

URL: https://www.insurance.ca.gov/0200-industry/0010-producer-online-services/0200-exam-info/

Colorado requires fingerprinting PRIOR to submitting your license application.

Fingerprints are submitted to the Colorado Bureau of Investigation (CBI) for state and federal (FBI) background check.

Vendor: IdentoGO
Fee: ~$49.50
URL: https://www.identogo.com

Alternative: Colorado Fingerprinting (for in-state applicants)

Processing time: 1-2 weeks

DOI info: https://doi.colorado.gov/colorado-and-national-fingerprint-based-criminal-history-background-check

Colorado does NOT require pre-licensing education for insurance producers.

Pre-licensing courses are recommended but optional to help prepare for the state exam.

Colorado insurance exams are administered by Pearson VUE.

Schedule online: https://www.pearsonvue.com/co/insurance
Phone: (800) 274-2616

Exams can be taken at a Pearson VUE test center or remotely via online proctoring (at home or office).

Exam fee: $48

Connecticut does NOT require fingerprinting for insurance license applicants.

A criminal background check is conducted as part of the application process, but fingerprints are not mandatory.

Contact: Connecticut Insurance Department
Phone: (860) 297-3845
Address: P.O. Box 816, Hartford, CT 06142-0816
override_description: No fingerprinting required; background check via application

instructions:
Connecticut requires 40 hours of pre-licensing education for a combined Life, Accident & Health license, or 20 hours for Life only.

Complete a state-approved course and pass the course final exam before scheduling the state licensing exam.
override_description: 20 hours Life only / 40 hours combined required

instructions:
Connecticut insurance exams are administered by Pearson VUE.

Schedule online: https://www.pearsonvue.com/us/en/ct/insurance.html
Phone: (866) 407-2057

Appointments may be made up to one calendar day prior to your desired test date, subject to availability.

75 questions, 120 minutes.

Washington D.C. requires fingerprinting for all insurance license applicants (both resident and nonresident).

Vendor: Fieldprint
Phone: (877) 614-4364
URL: https://www.fieldprint.com

Fingerprinting is required AFTER passing your exam and submitting your license application.

Fee: ~$50

Washington D.C. does NOT require pre-licensing education for insurance producers.

Pre-licensing courses are recommended but optional.

D.C. insurance exams are administered by Pearson VUE.

Schedule online: https://www.pearsonvue.com/us/en/dc/insurance.html
Phone: (800) 274-0610

Schedule at least 24 hours before your desired exam date. Pay by credit card, debit card, voucher, or electronic check (5 days advance for e-check).

Delaware requires a criminal history report within 90 days of application.

Schedule fingerprinting with the Delaware State Police.
Phone: (302) 739-2528
Fee: $75

After submitting your application, fax a copy of your criminal background reports to the Delaware Department of Insurance at: (302) 736-7906.
override_description: Fingerprinting required via DE State Police ($75)

instructions:
Delaware requires 40 hours of pre-licensing education for a combined Life, Accident & Health license, or 20 hours for Life only.

Complete a state-approved course before scheduling the state licensing exam.
override_description: 20 hours Life only / 40 hours combined required

instructions:
Delaware insurance exams are administered by Pearson VUE.

Schedule online: https://www.pearsonvue.com

Exams must be taken at a Pearson VUE test center (online exams not available). Testing centers in Woodside, Dover, Seaford, Wye Mills, and Middletown.

Life Producer exam: 106 questions, 2 hours 15 minutes.
override_description: Schedule exam via Pearson VUE (in-person only)

instructions:
Florida requires electronic fingerprinting for a criminal background check.

Vendor: IdentoGO
URL: https://www.identogo.com

Get fingerprinted AFTER passing your exam and BEFORE applying for your license.

Fingerprint records are valid for 1 year. If you hold a current license or were fingerprinted in the last 48 months, you may not need new prints (call 850-413-3134 to verify).

Contact FL DFS: (850) 413-3134

Florida requires 40 hours of pre-licensing education for a combined Life and Health license, or 20 hours for Life only (200-hour General Lines).

Complete a state-approved 40-hour or 200-hour course and pass the course final exam.

URL: https://www.myfloridacfo.com/division/agents/
override_description: 40 hours combined L&H / 200 hours General Lines required

instructions:
Florida insurance exams are administered by Pearson VUE.

Schedule online: https://www.pearsonvue.com/us/en/fl/insurance.html
Phone: (888) 274-2020

You can schedule before completing your pre-licensing course. Exam times available mornings, afternoons, and early evenings on weekdays.

Exam fee: $44. Passing score: 70%.

Georgia requires fingerprinting for all first-time resident insurance exam candidates.

Vendor: IdentoGO
Service Code: 2TGJ6B
URL: https://www.identogo.com

You must APPLY for a license BEFORE registering for fingerprinting. You will receive instructions after applying.

Fee: $51-$52.75

Georgia requires 40 hours of pre-licensing education for a combined Life, Accident & Sickness license, or 20 hours for each line separately.

You must pass the course final exam with 70% or higher to receive a certificate of completion.

URL: https://oci.georgia.gov/get-insurance-agent-license
override_description: 40 hours combined / 20 hours per line required

instructions:
Georgia insurance exams are administered by Pearson VUE.

Schedule online: https://www.pearsonvue.com/us/en/ga/insurance.html

Appointments may be made up to one calendar day prior to your desired test date, subject to availability.

2 hours to complete. Passing score: 70%.

Hawaii requires fingerprinting for all insurance license applicants.

Vendor: Fieldprint Hawaii
Code: HI-DCCA-INS
URL: https://www.fieldprintfingerprintservice.com

Schedule an appointment online and enter code HI-DCCA-INS to ensure the Insurance Division receives the prints.

Fee: $67

You must submit your license application within 60 days of fingerprinting.

Contact: Hawaii DCCA Insurance Division (808) 586-2788
override_description: Fingerprinting required via Fieldprint, code HI-DCCA-INS ($67)

instructions:
Hawaii requires 20 hours of pre-licensing education for Life insurance.

Complete a state-approved course before scheduling the state licensing exam.

URL: https://cca.hawaii.gov/ins/producers/exam_info/

Hawaii insurance exams are administered by Pearson VUE.

Schedule online: https://www.pearsonvue.com
Phone: (800) 274-2608

Exams available at test centers or via OnVUE online proctoring (remote). Walk-in testing is NOT available.

URL: https://cca.hawaii.gov/ins/producers/exam_info/

Effective June 2, 2025, Iowa requires fingerprinting for all resident insurance applicants.

Vendor: Fieldprint
URL: https://www.fieldprint.com

After applying through NIPR, you will receive an email with your Iowa Fieldprint code and instructions.

Fee: $34.35 (Iowa location) / $36.35 (out-of-state location)

Bring your appointment number and two forms of valid ID. Results reported within 3-5 business days.

Info: https://iid.iowa.gov/producer-fingerprinting-and-criminal-history-checks

Iowa does NOT require pre-licensing education for insurance producers.

Pre-licensing courses are recommended but optional.

Iowa insurance exams are administered by Pearson VUE.

Schedule online: https://www.pearsonvue.com/ia/insurance
Phone: (877) 540-5825

Schedule at least 24 hours before your desired exam date. Exams available at test centers or remotely via online proctoring.

Idaho requires fingerprinting for all resident insurance license applicants.

You can schedule fingerprinting at a Pearson VUE testing location on the same day as your exam. Schedule the 'InsID-FPELC' service with Pearson VUE.

Fee: $65
Vendor: Pearson VUE / IdentoGO

Processing time: 5-10 business days after fingerprinting, then 1-2 more days for license issuance.

URL: https://doi.idaho.gov/industry/licensing-services/testing-and-fingerprints/

Idaho does NOT require pre-licensing education for insurance producers.

Pre-licensing courses are recommended but optional.

Idaho insurance exams are administered by Pearson VUE.

Schedule online: https://www.pearsonvue.com/us/en/id/insurance.html
Phone: (800) 274-2721

Appointments may be made up to one calendar day prior. Life Producer exam: 101 questions, 2 hours 10 minutes.

Illinois requires fingerprinting within 60 days of your license application.

Fingerprints must be submitted through an Illinois-licensed live scan fingerprint vendor.

Vendor list: https://idfpr.illinois.gov/profs/fingerprint.html

Keep your fingerprint receipt as you may need it during the licensing process.

The Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation (IDFPR) maintains a list of licensed fingerprint vendors.
override_description: Fingerprinting required via IL-licensed live scan vendor

instructions:
Illinois requires 20 hours of pre-licensing education per line of authority.

Complete a state-approved 20-hour Life insurance course before scheduling the state exam.

URL: https://idfpr.illinois.gov

Illinois insurance exams are administered by Pearson VUE.

Schedule online: https://www.pearsonvue.com/us/en/il/insurance.html
Phone: (800) 274-0402

As of January 2025, remote state exams are NO LONGER offered - in-person only.

Life Producer General exam: 55 questions, 85 minutes. Life Producer State exam: 36 questions, 50 minutes.
override_description: Schedule exam via Pearson VUE (in-person only)

instructions:
Indiana does NOT generally require fingerprinting for Life insurance license applicants.

A background check is conducted as part of the application process through NIPR/Sircon.

Note: Property & Casualty applicants may have different fingerprint requirements.

Contact: Indiana Department of Insurance
URL: https://www.in.gov/idoi/
override_description: No fingerprinting required for Life; background check via application

instructions:
Indiana requires 40 hours of pre-licensing education for a Life and Health insurance license.

Complete a state-approved course before scheduling the state exam.

URL: https://www.in.gov/idoi/licensing/resident-licensing/examination-procedure-checklist/

Indiana insurance exams are administered by Pearson VUE in partnership with Ivy Tech Community College.

Schedule online: https://www.pearsonvue.com/in/insurance
Phone: (866) 895-0496

URL: https://www.in.gov/idoi/licensing/resident-licensing/examination-procedure-checklist/
override_description: Schedule exam via Pearson VUE / Ivy Tech

instructions:
Kansas requires fingerprinting for all unlicensed insurance license applicants.

FREE fingerprinting locations:
- Kansas Insurance Department office in Topeka
- Department of Children and Families (DCF) offices in Topeka, Overland Park, Wichita, Hutchinson, Salina, and Chanute
- Pearson VUE test centers in Overland Park, Topeka, and Wichita

Fingerprint + background check fee: $60 (collected with online application)
Application fee: $30

URL: https://insurance.ks.gov/documents/agentagency/Fingerprint-FAQ.pdf
override_description: Fingerprinting required - FREE at KS locations ($60 bg check fee)

instructions:
Kansas does NOT require pre-licensing education for insurance producers.

Pre-licensing courses are recommended but optional.

Kansas insurance exams are administered by Pearson VUE.

Schedule online: https://www.pearsonvue.com/us/en/ks/insurance.html
Phone: (888) 204-6255

Combined Life and Accident & Health exam: 156 questions (16 pretest), 2.5 hours. Passing score: 70%.

Kentucky requires a Criminal Background Report through the KY Administrative Office of the Courts (AOC).

The AOC report is valid for 60 days. Requests are typically processed within 24 hours (except weekends/holidays).

Note: Kentucky uses a court records check rather than fingerprint-based background check.

URL: https://insurance.ky.gov
override_description: Criminal background report via KY AOC required (no fingerprints)

instructions:
Kentucky requires 20 hours of pre-licensing education per line of authority.

Complete a state-approved classroom or self-study course.

URL: https://insurance.ky.gov/ppc/newstatic_info.aspx?static_id=72

Kentucky insurance exams are scheduled through the KY DOI eServices portal AFTER your license application is processed.

URL: https://insurance.ky.gov/doieservices

All applicants must have a scheduled appointment; walk-ins are NOT accepted. Kentucky does NOT offer remote testing.

Exam fee: $50. Changes must be made 24 hours prior. No-shows forfeit the $50 fee.
override_description: Schedule exam via KY DOI eServices ($50)

instructions:
Louisiana requires fingerprinting as part of the insurance licensing process.

You can submit fingerprints electronically at a PSI test site on the day of your exam (no separate appointment needed).

Alternatively, schedule through IdentoGO:
Service Code: 27N339
URL: https://www.identogo.com

Contact PSI or the Louisiana Department of Insurance for current fees.
override_description: Fingerprinting at PSI site on exam day or via IdentoGO (code 27N339)

instructions:
Louisiana requires 20 hours of pre-licensing education for Life insurance.

Complete a state-approved course before scheduling the state exam.

URL: https://www.ldi.la.gov

Louisiana insurance exams are administered by PSI.

Schedule online: https://www.psiexams.com
Phone: (800) 733-9267

Test centers in Baton Rouge, Lafayette, Lake Charles, Metairie, Monroe, Pineville (Alexandria), and Shreveport.

Series 101 - Life Insurance exam: 100 questions, 2 hours. Passing score: 70%.

Massachusetts requires fingerprinting as part of the insurance licensing process.

Fingerprinting is completed at Prometric after passing the exam.

Vendor: Prometric
URL: https://www.prometric.com
Phone: (800) 741-9380

Complete your license and fingerprints at the Prometric testing center.
override_description: Fingerprinting required at Prometric test center

instructions:
Massachusetts does NOT require pre-licensing education for insurance producers.

Pre-licensing courses are recommended but optional to help prepare for the state exam.

Massachusetts insurance exams are administered by Prometric.

Schedule online: https://www.prometric.com/exams/insurance-ma
Phone: (800) 741-9380

Exam times available mornings and afternoons on weekdays and certain weekends.

Life Insurance (Series 16-51) exam: 100 questions, 2 hours. Passing score: 70%.
Exam fee: $39.

Maryland does NOT require fingerprinting for insurance license applicants.

A background check is conducted as part of the application review process.

You can optionally register for fingerprinting at the same time you register for your exam, but it is not mandatory.

Contact: Maryland Insurance Administration
override_description: No fingerprinting required; background check via application

instructions:
Maryland requires 40 hours of pre-licensing education for a combined Life and Health license, or 20 hours for Life only.

Pre-licensing education is valid for 6 months from completion date. You must pass your state exam within this timeframe.
override_description: 20 hours Life only / 40 hours combined required

instructions:
Maryland insurance exams are administered by Prometric.

Schedule online: https://www.prometric.com
Phone: (800) 610-1174

Exams available at physical Prometric testing centers or remotely via online proctoring.

Life exam fee: $60.

Maine requires fingerprinting for all insurance license applicants.

Vendor: IdentoGO
URL: https://me.ibtfingerprint.com
Phone: (855) 667-7422

Register online, pay, and schedule a date, time, and location for your fingerprinting appointment.

Out-of-state applicants may use IdentoGO's Card Scan Processing Program.

Maine does NOT require pre-licensing education for insurance producers.

Pre-licensing courses are recommended but optional.

URL: https://www.maine.gov/pfr/insurance/licensees/individuals-business-entities/ce-exam-information

Maine insurance exams are administered by Pearson VUE.

Schedule online: https://www.pearsonvue.com/us/en/me/insurance.html
Phone: (800) 274-4959

As of May 2025, remote state exams are NO LONGER offered - in-person testing at Pearson VUE centers only.

Exam fee paid by credit/debit card, voucher, or electronic check at time of reservation.
override_description: Schedule exam via Pearson VUE (in-person only)

instructions:
Michigan does NOT require fingerprinting for insurance license applicants.

A background check is conducted as part of the application process. You must bring two valid forms of government-issued ID on exam day (one with photo and signature).

Contact: Michigan DIFS
URL: https://www.michigan.gov/difs
override_description: No fingerprinting required; background check via application

instructions:
Michigan requires 20 hours of pre-licensing education for Life insurance.

Certificate is valid for 12 months from completion date. You must pass the exam within this period.

URL: https://www.michigan.gov/difs/industry/licensing-ins/examinations

Michigan insurance exams are administered by PSI.

Schedule online: http://candidate.psiexams.com/DIFS
Phone: (800) 733-9267

Life insurance exam: 100 questions, 120 minutes.

URL: https://www.michigan.gov/difs/industry/licensing-ins/examinations

Minnesota requires fingerprinting and criminal history record check for all resident insurance producer applicants.

Recommended: Get fingerprinted electronically at a PSI Minnesota test site on the day of your exam.

Alternative: Any local police station with manual fingerprinting capability.

Fee: ~$46-$65 (varies by method and location)

Minnesota may issue a license before FBI background check results are returned.
override_description: Fingerprinting required at PSI site or local police (~$46-$65)

instructions:
Minnesota requires 20 hours of pre-licensing education for Life insurance.

Complete a state-approved course before scheduling the state exam.

URL: https://mn.gov/commerce/licensing/list/insurance/resident-producers.jsp

Minnesota insurance exams are administered by PSI / Pearson VUE.

PSI Phone: (866) 395-1006
Pearson VUE: https://home.pearsonvue.com/mncommerce
Pearson VUE Phone: (833) 273-1946

Fingerprinting is available at PSI test centers on exam day.
override_description: Schedule exam via PSI or Pearson VUE

instructions:
Missouri does NOT require fingerprinting for insurance license applicants.

A background check is initiated through the application process and reviewed during licensing.

Contact: Missouri Department of Commerce and Insurance
override_description: No fingerprinting required; background check via application

instructions:
Missouri does NOT require pre-licensing education for insurance producers.

Pre-licensing courses are recommended but optional.

Missouri insurance exams are administered by Pearson VUE.

Schedule online: https://www.pearsonvue.com
Phone: (866) 247-4740

Life Producer exam: 100 questions, 2 hours. Passing score: 70%.

Exam fee: $39 (single line) or $49 (combined Life, Health & Accident).

Mississippi requires fingerprinting for insurance license applicants.

Fingerprints are processed by the Mississippi Insurance Department (MID). Processing may take 2-6 weeks.

Fee: $50 (check or money order only)

Required documents:
- Recent headshot photo (no larger than 4x6)
- Social security card
- Valid driver's license

Contact: Mississippi Insurance Department
Phone: (601) 359-3582
URL: https://www.mid.ms.gov
override_description: Fingerprinting required via MID ($50, check/money order)

instructions:
Mississippi requires 20 hours of pre-licensing education for Life insurance.

Complete a state-approved course before scheduling the state exam.

URL: https://www.mid.ms.gov/mississippi-insurance-department/licensing/pre-licensing-and-continuing-education/

Mississippi insurance exams are administered by Pearson VUE.

Schedule online: https://www.pearsonvue.com/us/en/ms/insurance.html
Phone: (888) 293-4222

Hours: Monday-Friday, 7:00 AM - 7:00 PM CT.

URL: https://www.mid.ms.gov/mississippi-insurance-department/licensing/licensing-examinations/

Montana requires fingerprinting for all first-time insurance license applicants and those adding a line of authority.

Vendor: IdentoGO (IDEMIA)
URL: https://www.identogo.com
Phone: (866) 761-8069

Schedule your appointment through IdentoGO AFTER taking your exam. Fingerprints can also be scheduled through Pearson VUE's third-party vendor.

Fee: $54

Fingerprints are valid for 1 year.

URL: https://csimt.gov/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/Fingerprints-for-Insurance-Licensing-Purposes.pdf

Montana requires 20 hours of pre-licensing education for Life insurance.

Complete a state-approved course before scheduling the state exam.

URL: https://csimt.gov/insurance/licensing/

Montana insurance exams are administered by Pearson VUE.

Schedule online: https://www.pearsonvue.com/mt/insurance
Phone: (866) 274-8906

Schedule at least 24 hours before your desired exam date.

Life exam: 102 questions (92 scored, 10 pretest), 135 minutes. Passing score: 75%.
Exam fee: $59.
override_description: Schedule exam via Pearson VUE ($59, 75% to pass)

instructions:
North Carolina requires fingerprinting within 30 days of applying for a license.

Process:
1. Submit your license application through NIPR
2. Print the Fingerprint Submission Packet from the NIPR email link
3. Take the packet to a local law enforcement agency for digital fingerprints (Live Scan)
4. Applicant completes pages 1 and 3; law enforcement signs page 2

Fingerprint cards are NOT accepted - must be electronic Live Scan.

Fee: $38

URL: https://www.ncdoi.gov/licensees/agent-and-adjuster-licensing/become-insurance-agent-or-adjuster/resident-fingerprint-requirements
override_description: Fingerprinting required via local law enforcement Live Scan ($38)

instructions:
North Carolina requires 20 hours of pre-licensing education for Life insurance.

Complete a state-approved course and pass the final exam with 70% or higher.

URL: https://www.ncdoi.gov

North Carolina insurance exams are administered by Pearson VUE.

After filing your application through NIPR, you will receive an email from Pearson VUE with exam scheduling instructions (within 1 hour).

Schedule at least 24 hours before your desired exam date.

Life Agent exam: 60 questions, 1 hour 15 minutes.
Exam fee: $45.

North Dakota requires fingerprinting for all initial resident insurance producer applicants.

Fingerprints may be taken at PSI test centers on the day of your exam, or at other locations.

Fees:
- PSI fingerprint processing: $29
- ND Attorney General background check: $40 (check or money order)

Total: ~$69

Contact: ND Insurance Department Producer Licensing
Phone: (701) 328-2440
Email: ndlicensing@nd.gov
override_description: Fingerprinting required at PSI or other locations (~$69)

instructions:
North Dakota requires 20 hours of pre-licensing education for Life insurance.

Complete a state-approved course before scheduling the state exam.

URL: https://www.insurance.nd.gov/producers/resident-producer

North Dakota insurance exams are administered by PSI.

Schedule online: https://www.psiexams.com
Phone: (855) 340-3905

Exams available at physical PSI centers or remotely via online proctoring.

Nebraska requires fingerprinting and a background check as part of the insurance licensing process.

Complete fingerprinting after passing the exam and before submitting your license application through NIPR.

Contact the Nebraska Department of Insurance for current vendor and fee information.
Phone: (402) 471-2201
URL: https://doi.nebraska.gov
override_description: Fingerprinting required; contact NE DOI for vendor details

instructions:
Nebraska does NOT require pre-licensing education for insurance producers.

Pre-licensing courses are recommended but optional.

URL: https://doi.nebraska.gov/education-licensees

Nebraska insurance exams are administered by PSI.

Schedule online: https://www.psiexams.com
Phone: (833) 333-4754

Test centers in Omaha, Lincoln, Norfolk, Grand Island, North Platte, Sidney, and Scottsbluff. Remote testing also available.

Life and Health exam: 100 questions, 2 hours. Passing score: 70%.

New Hampshire requires a criminal history check for insurance license applicants.

Schedule through New Hampshire's Office of Professional Licensure & Certification.

Fee: $25 (payable online by credit or debit card)

Contact: NH Insurance Department License Division
Phone: (603) 271-0203

New Hampshire does NOT require pre-licensing education for insurance producers.

Pre-licensing courses are recommended but optional.

New Hampshire insurance exams are administered by PSI (effective June 2025, replacing Prometric).

Schedule online: https://www.psiexams.com

Note: Prometric ceased scheduling NH exams after June 23, 2025. PSI began accepting registrations June 30, 2025.

Life exam: 100 questions, 120 minutes.
override_description: Schedule exam via PSI (replaced Prometric June 2025)

instructions:
New Jersey requires fingerprinting for all insurance license applicants.

Vendor: IdentoGO
Service Code: 2F16Y3
URL: https://www.identogo.com
Phone: (877) 503-5981

Fee: $66.05

Submit your license application within 1 year of passing the exam.

New Jersey does NOT require pre-licensing education for insurance producers.

Pre-licensing courses are recommended but optional.

New Jersey insurance exams are administered by PSI.

Schedule online: https://www.psiexams.com
Phone: (855) 579-4634

Schedule at least 2 days in advance. Cancel/reschedule at least 48 hours before appointment.

Life exam: 88 questions, 3.5 hours. Passing score: 70%.

New Mexico requires fingerprinting BEFORE submitting your license application.

Vendor: IdentoGO (via NM Dept of Public Safety)
URL: https://www.identogo.com

All applicants must register online prior to being fingerprinted. Fingerprints are run through NM DPS and FBI.

Fee: $59

Wait 3-5 days after fingerprinting before submitting your application.
override_description: Fingerprinting required via IdentoGO BEFORE applying ($59)

instructions:
New Mexico does NOT require pre-licensing education for insurance producers.

Pre-licensing courses are recommended but optional.

URL: https://www.osi.state.nm.us

New Mexico insurance exams are administered by PSI.

Schedule online: https://www.psiexams.com
Phone: (855) 560-3714

Exams available in-person or remotely.

Life Insurance exam: 100 questions, 2 hours. Passing score: 70%.

Nevada requires fingerprinting for all insurance license applicants.

Vendor: IdentoGO
URL: https://www.identogo.com
Phone: (866) 761-8069

Fee: $60

Keep the original receipt and attach a copy to your license application along with the Fingerprint Background Waiver.

Submit your license application within 1 year of passing the exam.

URL: https://doi.nv.gov/Licensing/License_Types/Producer/Individuals/

Nevada requires 20 hours of pre-licensing education for Life insurance.

Complete a state-approved course before scheduling the state exam.

URL: https://doi.nv.gov

Nevada insurance exams are administered by Pearson VUE.

Schedule online: https://www.pearsonvue.com
Phone: (800) 274-2609

Test centers in Las Vegas, Reno, and Elko.

Life exam: 97 questions. Passing score: 80% (higher than most states).
Exam fee: $39.
override_description: Schedule exam via Pearson VUE ($39, 80% to pass)

instructions:
New York requires fingerprinting for insurance license applicants.

Vendor: IdentoGO
URL: https://www.identogo.com
Phone: (877) 472-6915

IMPORTANT: DFS must receive your application within 10 days of fingerprint scans, or scans will be discarded and you must restart (including repaying the fee).

Required documents to submit to DFS:
- Two completed fingerprint cards
- Signed IdentoGO pre-enrollment confirmation/receipt
- Fingerprint Certification Form
- Request for Fingerprint Card Scan Services form

Contact: NY DFS (800) 342-3736
URL: https://www.dfs.ny.gov/apps_and_licensing/fingerprinting_procedure
override_description: Fingerprinting required via IdentoGO (apply within 10 days)

instructions:
New York requires 40 hours of pre-licensing education for Life, Accident & Health combined.

Complete a state-approved course with an approved provider.

URL: https://www.dfs.ny.gov/apps_and_licensing/agents_and_brokers/home

New York insurance exams are administered by PSI Services LLC.

Schedule online: https://www.psiexams.com
Phone: (855) 579-4637

Exams available at physical PSI centers or remotely via online proctoring.

Life exam: 100 questions, 120 minutes.

Ohio requires fingerprinting per Ohio Revised Code 3905.051 for all resident insurance license applicants.

Schedule through any vendor on the Ohio Attorney General Webcheck Portal.

Fee: $72.25

Fingerprints are processed by the Ohio Bureau of Criminal Investigation (BCI) and FBI.

Background check results valid for 1 year. Submit your license application within 1 year of passing the exam.

URL: https://www.ohioattorneygeneral.gov/Business/Services-for-Business/WebCheck
override_description: Fingerprinting required via OH AG Webcheck ($72.25)

instructions:
Ohio requires 20 hours of pre-licensing education for Life insurance.

Complete a state-approved course before scheduling the state exam.

Ohio insurance exams are administered by PSI.

Schedule online: https://home.psiexams.com (select OH Insurance)
Phone: Check PSI website

Retakes available as soon as 24 hours after first attempt.

Life exam: 100 questions, 120 minutes. Passing score: 70%.
Exam fee: $42.

Oklahoma requires a background check as part of the insurance licensing process. Fingerprinting requirements may apply.

Complete the background check after passing your exam.

Allow 3 business days for PSI to send exam results to the Oklahoma Insurance Department, then 5 business days for OID to review your application.

Contact: Oklahoma Insurance Department
Phone: (405) 521-3916
URL: https://www.oid.ok.gov
override_description: Background check required; contact OID for current fingerprint requirements

instructions:
Oklahoma does NOT require pre-licensing education for insurance producers.

Pre-licensing courses are recommended but optional.

Oklahoma insurance exams are administered by PSI.

Schedule online: https://www.psiexams.com
Phone: (833) 333-4754

Exams available at physical PSI centers or remotely via online proctoring.

URL: https://www.oid.ok.gov/oklahoma-insurance-licensing-exam/

Oregon requires fingerprinting for all resident insurance license applicants.

Option 1 - PSI Oregon sites (walk-in, no appointment needed):
- Available during regular testing hours on exam day
- Fee: $61.25
- Locations: https://dfr.oregon.gov/business/licensing/insurance/Documents/psi-fingerprint-locations.pdf

Option 2 - Fieldprint (appointment required):
- URL: https://www.fieldprintoregon.com

Both vendors digitally capture and transmit prints to Oregon State Police and FBI.

URL: https://dfr.oregon.gov/business/licensing/insurance/pages/fingerprint.aspx
override_description: Fingerprinting required via PSI walk-in ($61.25) or Fieldprint

instructions:
Oregon requires 20 hours of pre-licensing education for Life insurance.

Complete a state-approved course before scheduling the state exam.

URL: https://dfr.oregon.gov/business/licensing/insurance/pages/producer-licensing.aspx

Oregon insurance exams are administered by PSI.

Schedule online: https://www.psiexams.com
Phone: (800) 733-9267

Both test center and online remote-proctored testing available. Complete pre-licensing education first and have your Certificate of Completion ready.

Contact: Producer Licensing (503) 947-7981 or web.insagent@oregon.gov

Pennsylvania requires fingerprinting for all insurance license applicants.

Vendor: IdentoGO
Service Code: 1KGBGJ
URL: https://www.identogo.com
Phone: (844) 321-2101

Do NOT register for fingerprinting until AFTER you have passed the exam and applied for licensure.

Payment at IdentoGO center after fingerprints are submitted. Credit card, debit card, certified check, or money order only (no cash or personal checks).

Pennsylvania requires 24 hours of pre-licensing education for Life insurance.

Complete a state-approved course before scheduling the state exam.

URL: https://www.pa.gov/agencies/insurance/licensing/licensees/initial-insurance-producer-licensing-process

Pennsylvania insurance exams are administered by PSI.

Schedule online: https://www.psiexams.com
Phone: (888) 818-5822

Remote exams available via PSI Bridge, or in-person at PSI testing sites.

Life exam: 100 questions, 2 hours. Passing score: 70%.

Rhode Island requires fingerprinting for insurance license applicants.

Location: Attorney General Customer Service Center
Address: 4 Howard Avenue, Cranston, RI 02920

No appointments - register when you arrive (walk-in only).

Fee: $5

Background reports valid for 30 days.

Questions: bcifingerprints@riag.ri.gov (fingerprinting) or BCIstate@riag.ri.gov (background checks)
override_description: Fingerprinting required at RI AG office, walk-in ($5)

instructions:
Rhode Island requires 20 hours of pre-licensing education for Life insurance.

Complete a state-approved course before scheduling the state exam.

Rhode Island insurance exams are administered by Pearson VUE.

Schedule online: https://www.pearsonvue.com
Phone: (800) 274-3739

Exams available at Pearson VUE test centers or via remote online proctored exam.

Reschedule at least 48 hours prior or forfeit exam fee. Bring two valid forms of government-issued ID.

South Carolina requires fingerprinting for all resident insurance license applicants.

Vendor: IdentoGO
URL: https://www.identogo.com

After applying for a resident producer license, you will receive an email with directions to schedule a fingerprint appointment through IdentoGO.

Fee: $50.50

Fingerprints are compared against SLED (statewide) and FBI (national) criminal history records.

URL: https://doi.sc.gov/879/Fingerprinting

South Carolina requires 20 hours of pre-licensing education for Life insurance.

Complete a state-approved course before scheduling the state exam.

URL: https://doi.sc.gov/481/Producer

Schedule online: https://www.pearsonvue.com
Phone: (877) 759-5226

URL: https://doi.sc.gov/481/Producer

South Dakota requires fingerprinting for certain insurance license types. The requirement may vary by license type.

Fingerprints are submitted through a Livescan machine at a South Dakota location. You must print a receipt and present it at the Livescan location.

Fee: $50 (State and FBI check) or $30 (State only)

Processed by: SD Division of Criminal Investigation Identification Section (DCI ID)

Contact: South Dakota Division of Insurance
Phone: (605) 773-3513
URL: https://dlr.sd.gov/insurance
override_description: Fingerprinting may be required via Livescan ($50)

instructions:
South Dakota does NOT require pre-licensing education for insurance producers.

Pre-licensing courses are recommended but optional.

South Dakota insurance exams are administered by Pearson VUE.

Schedule online: https://www.pearsonvue.com
Phone: (800) 274-5993

Remote exams available via OnVUE. Exam scores valid for 180 days.

120 minutes to complete. Exam fee: $85.

Tennessee requires fingerprinting for all resident producer applicants.

Vendor: IdentoGO
ORI: TN920680Z
Transaction Type: IP (TN Insurance Producer)
URL: https://www.identogo.com

Enter: Name, Agency (Department of Commerce and Insurance), ORI # TN920680Z, Transaction Type IP.

Fee: $42

Submit fingerprints at least 2 business days BEFORE submitting your license application.
override_description: Fingerprinting required via IdentoGO, ORI TN920680Z ($42)

instructions:
Tennessee does NOT require pre-licensing education for insurance producers.

Pre-licensing courses are recommended but optional.

Tennessee insurance exams are administered by Pearson VUE.

Schedule online: https://www.pearsonvue.com
Phone: (800) 274-4957

10-day waiting period to reschedule. After subsequent attempts, must wait 30 days before retaking.

Texas requires fingerprinting for most insurance license applicants.

Vendor: IdentoGO
Scheduling Code: 11G6QF
URL: https://www.identogo.com
Phone: (888) 467-2080

Fee: $39.70

Send a copy of the IdentoGO receipt (showing prints sent to TX DPS) with your application.

Fingerprint results valid for 1 year. Exemption: active TX licensees who already submitted prints.

Texas does NOT require pre-licensing education for Life insurance producers.

Pre-licensing courses are recommended but optional.

Texas insurance exams are administered by Pearson VUE.

Phone: (888) 754-7667

Register at least 24 hours in advance. Submit your application within 1 year of passing the exam.

Utah requires fingerprinting for all resident insurance license applicants.

Fingerprinting is done at a Prometric test center AFTER passing the exam. The license application (completed at a kiosk at the test center) includes the fingerprint fee.

Fee: $38 total ($12 FBI + $20 BCI + $6 Prometric processing)

Pay by credit card during the online application process.

Processing time: 5-10 business days (allow 30 days for BCI/FBI to process).

URL: https://insurance.utah.gov/licensees/producers/exam/

Utah does NOT require pre-licensing education for insurance producers.

Pre-licensing courses are recommended but optional.

Utah insurance exams are administered by Prometric.

Schedule online: https://www.prometric.com
Phone: (888) 226-8740

Exams taken at Prometric test centers. After passing, apply at a kiosk at the test center.

URL: https://insurance.utah.gov/licensees/producers/exam/

Virginia requires electronic fingerprinting for a criminal background check.

Vendor: Fieldprint
Fieldprint Code: FPVABOICHRR
URL: https://www.fieldprint.com
Phone: (877) 614-4364

Fee: $34.95

Fingerprints must be no older than 90 days when used with an application. Submit your application within 90 days of fingerprinting.

instructions:
Virginia requires 20 hours of pre-licensing education for Life insurance.

Complete a state-approved course before scheduling the state exam.

Virginia insurance exams are administered by Prometric.

Schedule online: https://www.prometric.com

Exams available at Prometric physical testing locations or remotely via ProProctor application.

Exam fee: $35. Prometric registration valid for 90 days (must pass within that window).

Vermont has minimal fingerprinting/background check requirements for insurance licenses.

Vermont is one of the states that may not require fingerprinting or a formal background check for insurance licensing. However, you may be asked to submit fingerprints at Prometric after passing the exam.

Contact the Vermont Department of Financial Regulation for current requirements.

URL: https://dfr.vermont.gov
override_description: Minimal requirements; contact VT DFR for current details

instructions:
Vermont does NOT require pre-licensing education for insurance producers.

Pre-licensing courses are recommended but optional.

Vermont insurance exams are administered by Prometric.

Schedule online: https://www.prometric.com/exams/insurance-vt

Exams can be taken online or in person at a Prometric test center.

Life and Accident, Health, and HMO exams are both 2 hours long.

Wait 48 hours after passing before applying. Application fee: $60.

Washington requires fingerprinting for all insurance license applicants.

Vendor: IdentoGO (IDEMIA)
URL: https://www.identogo.com
Phone: (888) 771-5097

You cannot schedule fingerprinting until the OIC has received your license application. You may schedule fingerprinting for the day of your exam at the exam site.

Fee: $49

Fingerprints sent to Washington State Patrol, which forwards results to the insurance commissioner.

URL: https://www.insurance.wa.gov/producers-adjusters/licensing/get-licensed/fingerprint-requirements

Washington requires 20 hours of pre-licensing education for Life insurance.

Complete a state-approved course before scheduling the state exam.

URL: https://www.insurance.wa.gov

Washington insurance exams are administered by PSI.

Schedule online: https://www.psiexams.com
Phone: (855) 205-5825

10 PSI test centers throughout Washington state. Remote testing also available.

Passing score: 70%.

Wisconsin requires fingerprinting for all unlicensed insurance license applicants.

Fieldprint Code: FPWIOCIInsurance (not case sensitive)

The Wisconsin OCI requires fingerprinting to ensure applicants do not have a disqualifying criminal or social services background.

West Virginia requires fingerprinting for all initial resident insurance license applicants.

Service Code: 228NS5
URL: https://www.identogo.com

Schedule AFTER passing the state exam.

Fingerprints transmitted to WV State Police and FBI. Results valid for 30 days only - act quickly on next steps.

West Virginia requires 20 hours of pre-licensing education per line of authority (Life, Accident & Sickness, Property, Casualty, Personal Lines).

Phone: (800) 274-2614

Schedule at least 24 hours in advance. Cancel/reschedule up to 48 hours before.

Wyoming requires fingerprinting for all first-time resident insurance license applicants.

After your license application is received, you will be MAILED a fingerprinting packet. Do NOT submit fingerprints before applying.

Make arrangements with your local law enforcement to obtain fingerprints. There may be a fee charged by law enforcement.

Fee: $39 (applies to all initial resident producer applications)

Processing time: 2-3 weeks from when the department receives your fingerprints.

URL: https://doi.wyo.gov/licensing/producers
override_description: Fingerprinting via local law enforcement after mailed packet ($39)

instructions:
Wyoming does NOT require pre-licensing education for insurance producers.
